skills.name: Programming Language (Kotlin/Java)
skills.description: Core languages used for Android development, with Kotlin being the modern preferred choice.
skills.notes: Learn Kotlin fundamentals, OOP concepts, null safety, coroutines, and Java basics if needed. Kotlin is officially recommended by Google.

skills.name: Android Fundamentals
skills.description: Basic concepts required to start building Android apps, including project setup and Gradle.
skills.notes: Covers Android Studio setup, Gradle build system, project structure, and creating your first app.

skills.name: Version Control (Git)
skills.description: Managing code changes and collaboration using Git and platforms like GitHub.
skills.notes: Learn commits, branching, merging, and collaboration workflows using GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.

skills.name: Android App Components
skills.description: Core building blocks of Android applications.
skills.notes: Includes Activities, Intents, Services, Broadcast Receivers, and Content Providers. Understand lifecycle and communication between components.

skills.name: UI Development (XML & Jetpack Compose)
skills.description: Designing user interfaces using traditional XML layouts and modern Jetpack Compose.
skills.notes: Covers layouts (ConstraintLayout), views, fragments, animations, and Compose for declarative UI development.

skills.name: Navigation & User Interaction
skills.description: Handling screen transitions and user interactions within the app.
skills.notes: Includes Navigation Components, fragments, back stack management, and deep linking.

skills.name: Architecture & Design Patterns
skills.description: Structuring apps using scalable and maintainable architectural patterns.
skills.notes: Includes MVVM, MVI, MVC, repository pattern, and clean architecture principles.

skills.name: Data Storage & Persistence
skills.description: Managing local data storage within Android apps.
skills.notes: Includes SharedPreferences, DataStore, Room database, file storage, and caching strategies.

skills.name: Networking
skills.description: Communicating with external APIs and services.
skills.notes: Includes REST APIs, Retrofit, OkHttp, JSON parsing, and GraphQL (Apollo Android).

skills.name: Asynchronism, Testing & Deployment
skills.description: Handling background tasks, testing apps, and distributing them to users.
skills.notes: Includes coroutines, RxJava, WorkManager, unit/UI testing (JUnit, Espresso), debugging tools, and publishing apps via Google Play Store.
